text: Surface strain or compression/stretch of surface atoms of nanoparticles (NPs) can be controlled to change the surface chemistry and enhance the catalytic activity of NPs for various chemical reactions. Here we report that the surface strain of Pt in the bimetallic core/shell structure FePt/Pt with three Pt layers can be rationally regulated by changing the structure of core (feet) from the cubic solid solution of face centered cubic (fcc) to the tetragonal structure of face centered tetragonal (fct). The significantly enhanced catalytic activity of the fct-FePt/Pt NPs for oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) is attributed to that fct-FePt relieves the over-compressed Pt strain in fcc-FePt according to the quantum mechanics-molecular mechanics (QM-MM) simulations. Furthermore, the Pt strain over catalytic activity of ORR can be enhanced by partially replacing Fe with Cu in FePt. Accordingly, fct-FeCuPt/Pt NPs show the highest catalytic activity for ORR than those of other five NPs and the commercial Pt catalyst. Thus, the structure-induced surface strain for ORR and many other chemical reactions could be regulated and optimized for catalytic activity.
